content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA                                    EDMUND G. BROWN JR., Governor

CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY COMMISSION
1120 N STREET, P.O. BOX 1139
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95805

Francis Sarquis, Commission Member
P.O. Box 816
Santa Barbara, CA 93102

OPINION REQUEST
CV 76/128 IL

May 26, 1976


Office of Attorney General
555 Capitol Mall
Sacramento, CA 95814

FOR CONSIDERATION
WEEK ENDING 7/16/76

[illegible] at May Concern:    Dear Mr. [illegible]:

Nearly two months ago, I was appointed by Governor Brown to the
California Highway Commission.

I am an attorney by occupation, sole practitioner, with a general
practice. Among my various clients, I have one which is a public
agency, to wit, the Santa Barbara Metropolitan Transit District
(SBMTD).

I have rendered legal services to the SBMTD on an hourly basis, ever
since the SBMTD was created some ten years ago. The District operates
pursuant to the enabling legislation found under Public Utilities Code
§595000 et seq., and the nature of my legal services to the Board is
more particularly described in §95533. I estimate that I render between
8 and 15 hours of service per month to the SBMTD. My other legal work
ranges over many areas, such as domestic relations, probate law, etc.

As a result of my appointment to the State Highway Commission, one of
the SBMTD Board members has asked whether my Commission appointment
poses any conflict of interest problem with my legal work for the
SBMTD. I personally considered this matter prior to receiving the
appointment and came to a negative conclusion. The same question was
also discussed with Secretary Donald Burns of the business and Trans-
portation Agency at the time he considered my appointment. He likewise
came up with a negative conclusion. Lastly, since the matter was
brought up by one of our SBMTD Board members, I have also raised this
question with Kingsley Hoegstedt, who is legal counsel for the Highway
Commission. His conclusion was likewise negative.

As Mr. Hoegstedt points out, I am not occupying an office with the
SBMTD; in a manner of speaking, I am something akin to an "employee."
Thus, although I hold no "office" at the SBMTD, the Board would
nonetheless like confirmation that no conflict of interest exists. Accordingly,
I wish to solicit from you a letter-opinion on this question. If I
can be of any assistance in providing any other information which
you deem pertinent to the inquiry, I am of course prepared to assist
in any way I can.

Sincerely,

[signature]
Francis Sarquis

PS:cll

embedding.qa_text: This letter is from Francis Sarguis, a Commission Member of the California Highway Commission, to the Office of Attorney General regarding a potential conflict of interest. Here's a summary of the content: Francis Sarguis was appointed by Governor Brown to the California Highway Commission. He is an attorney with a general practice and has provided legal services to the Santa Barbara Metropolitan Transit District (SBMTD) on an hourly basis since its creation ten years ago. The nature of his legal services to SBMTD is described in §95533, and he estimates rendering between 8 and 15 hours of service per month. One of the SBMTD Board members asked whether Sarguis' Commission appointment poses any conflict of interest problem with his legal work for SBMTD. Sarguis personally considered this matter prior to receiving the appointment and came to a negative conclusion. This question was also discussed with Secretary Donald Burns of the Business and Transportation Agency at the time he considered Sarguis' appointment, who likewise came up with a negative conclusion. Lastly, Sarguis raised this question with Kingsley Hoegstedt, who is legal counsel for the Highway Commission, and his conclusion was likewise negative. Sarguis notes that he is not occupying an office with SBMTD; in a manner of speaking, he is something akin to an "employee." Thus, although he holds no "office" at SBMTD, the Board would nevertheless like confirmation that no conflict exists. Accordingly, Sarguis wishes to solicit from the Attorney General a letter-opinion on this question. If he can be of any assistance in providing any other information which you deem pertinent to the inquiry, he is prepared to assist in any way he can. Sincerely, Francis Sarguis FS:cll
